tools: # Initialize directories and create test source files mkdir -p /src /build /dist # Create test source files cat > /src/main.c << 'EOF' #include <stdio.h> int main() { printf("Hello from main\n"); return 0; } EOF cat > /src/utils.c << 'EOF' #include <stdio.h> void print_util() { printf("Utility function\n"); } EOF echo "=== BUILD PIPELINE SIMULATION ===" echo "" # Stage 1: Check source files echo "[STAGE 1/4] Checking source files..." if [ -f /src/main.c ] && [ -f /src/utils.c ]; then echo "✓ Source files found: main.c, utils.c" echo "✓ STAGE 1 PASSED" else echo "✗ ERROR: Source files not found" exit 1 fi echo "" # Stage 2: Compile echo "[STAGE 2/4] Compiling source files..." compilation_failed=0 for src_file in /src/*.c; do filename=$(basename "$src_file") obj_file="/build/${filename%.c}.o" # Prepend '// compiled' and copy to build directory if (echo "// compiled" && cat "$src_file") > "$obj_file"; then echo "✓ Compiled: $filename → $(basename $obj_file)" else echo "✗ ERROR: Failed to compile $filename" compilation_failed=1 break fi done if [ $compilation_failed -eq 0 ]; then echo "✓ STAGE 2 PASSED" else echo "✗ STAGE 2 FAILED" exit 1 fi echo "" # Stage 3: Link echo "[STAGE 3/4] Linking object files..." obj_files=(/build/*.o) if [ ${#obj_files[@]} -eq 0 ]; then echo "✗ ERROR: No object files found in /build/" exit 1 fi # Create linked program with header if (echo "// linked" && cat /build/*.o) > /build/program; then echo "✓ Linked $(ls /build/*.o | wc -l) object file(s) → program" echo "✓ STAGE 3 PASSED" else echo "✗ ERROR: Failed to link files" exit 1 fi echo "" # Stage 4: Package echo "[STAGE 4/4] Packaging build artifacts..." if tar -czf /dist/release.tar.gz -C /build . 2>/dev/null; then tar_size=$(ls -lh /dist/release.tar.gz | awk '{print $5}') echo "✓ Created package: release.tar.gz ($tar_size)" echo "✓ STAGE 4 PASSED" else echo "✗ ERROR: Failed to create tar.gz package" exit 1 fi echo "" echo "=== BUILD PIPELINE COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY ===" echo "" echo "Summary:" echo " Stage 1 (Check): ✓ PASSED" echo " Stage 2 (Compile): ✓ PASSED" echo " Stage 3 (Link): ✓ PASSED" echo " Stage 4 (Package): ✓ PASSED" echo "" echo "Output files:" ls -lh /build/ /dist/ , echo "=== VERIFICATION OF BUILD ARTIFACTS ===" echo "" echo "--- Contents of /build/main.o ---" head -5 /build/main.o echo "" echo "--- Contents of /build/utils.o ---" head -5 /build/utils.o echo "" echo "--- Contents of /build/program (first 10 lines) ---" head -10 /build/program echo "" echo "--- Package contents ---" tar -tzf /dist/release.tar.gz

/src/utils.c << 'EOF'\n#include <stdio.h>\nvoid print_util() {\n printf(\"Utility function\\n\");\n}\nEOF\n\necho \"=== BUILD PIPELINE SIMULATION ===\"\necho \"\"\n\n# Stage 1: Check source Excellent! Here's a summary of the build pipeline simulation:

## Build Pipeline Results

### ✓ **All Stages Passed**

| Stage | Action | Result |
|-------|--------|--------|
| **1. Check** | Verified `/src/main.c` and `/src/utils.c` exist | ✓ Found both files |
| **2. Compile** | Created `.o` files with `// compiled` header | ✓ Generated `main.o` and `utils.o` |
| **3. Link** | Concatenated `.o` files with `// linked` header | ✓ Created `/build/program` |
| **4. Package** | Created tar.gz archive of build directory | ✓ Generated `/dist/release.tar.gz` |

### Output Files Created
- `/build/main.o` - Compiled main source (93 bytes)
- `/build/utils.o` - Compiled utilities source (87 bytes)
- `/build/program` - Final linked executable (190 bytes)
- `/dist/release.tar.gz` - Packaged release (283 bytes)

### Key Features
- **Error Handling**: Each stage checks for success and exits immediately on failure
- **Status Reporting**: Clear ✓/✗ indicators for each operation
- **Artifact Marking**: Both compiled files have `// compiled` header, linked program has `// linked` header
- **Clean Summary**: Final report shows all stages passed with file listings

tools: # Setup: create the source files and required directories mkdir -p /src /build /dist cat > /src/main.c << 'EOF' #include <stdio.h> int main() { printf("Hello from main!\n"); return 0; } EOF cat > /src/utils.c << 'EOF' #include <stdio.h> void util_hello() { printf("Hello from utils!\n"); } EOF echo "Source files created." , cat << 'PIPELINE_EOF' > /home/eval/build_pipeline.sh #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail # ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── GREEN='\033[0;32m' RED='\033[0;31m' YELLOW='\033[1;33m' CYAN='\033[0;36m' BOLD='\033[1m' RESET='\033[0m' STAGE_NUM=0 stage_header() { STAGE_NUM=$((STAGE_NUM + 1)) echo "" echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${RESET}" echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD} Stage ${STAGE_NUM}: $1${RESET}" echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${RESET}" } ok() { echo -e " ${GREEN}✔ $*${RESET}"; } info() { echo -e " ${YELLOW}➜ $*${RESET}"; } fail() { echo "" echo -e " ${RED}${BOLD}✘ ERROR — $*${RESET}" echo -e " ${RED}${BOLD}Pipeline aborted.${RESET}" echo "" exit 1 } summary() { echo "" echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${RESET}" echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD} ✔ BUILD PIPELINE COMPLETE${RESET}" echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${RESET}" echo "" } # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Stage 1 — Check sources # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ stage_header "Check Sources" REQUIRED_FILES=("/src/main.c" "/src/utils.c") for f in "${REQUIRED_FILES[@]}"; do if [[ -f "$f" ]]; then SIZE=$(wc -c < "$f") ok "Found $f (${SIZE} bytes)" else fail "Required source file not found: $f" fi done # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Stage 2 — Compile (copy → /build/*.o, prepend header) # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ stage_header "Compile" mkdir -p /build OBJECT_FILES=() for src in "${REQUIRED_FILES[@]}"; do base=$(basename "$src" .c) obj="/build/${base}.o" info "Compiling $src → $obj" { echo "// compiled" cat "$src" } > "$obj" LINES=$(wc -l < "$obj") ok "Written $obj (${LINES} lines)" OBJECT_FILES+=("$obj") done # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Stage 3 — Link (concatenate .o files → /build/program) # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ stage_header "Link" PROGRAM="/build/program" info "Linking ${#OBJECT_FILES[@]} object file(s) → $PROGRAM" { echo "// linked" for obj in "${OBJECT_FILES[@]}"; do echo "" echo "// --- $obj ---" cat "$obj" done } > "$PROGRAM" TOTAL_LINES=$(wc -l < "$PROGRAM") ok "Linked binary written: $PROGRAM (${TOTAL_LINES} lines total)" for obj in "${OBJECT_FILES[@]}"; do ok " included: $obj" done # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Stage 4 — Package (/build/ → /dist/release.tar.gz) # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ stage_header "Package" mkdir -p /dist ARCHIVE="/dist/release.tar.gz" info "Creating archive → $ARCHIVE" tar -czf "$ARCHIVE" -C / build/ \ || fail "tar exited with code $? — packaging failed" ARCHIVE_SIZE=$(wc -c < "$ARCHIVE") ok "Archive created: $ARCHIVE (${ARCHIVE_SIZE} bytes)" info "Archive contents:" tar -tzf "$ARCHIVE" | while read -r entry; do echo -e " ${YELLOW}${entry}${RESET}" done # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Done # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ summary PIPELINE_EOF chmod +x /home/eval/build_pipeline.sh bash /home/eval/build_pipeline.sh , mkdir -p /home/eval, cat << 'PIPELINE_EOF' > /home/eval/build_pipeline.sh #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail # ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── GREEN='\033[0;32m' RED='\033[0;31m' YELLOW='\033[1;33m' CYAN='\033[0;36m' BOLD='\033[1m' RESET='\033[0m' STAGE_NUM=0 stage_header() { STAGE_NUM=$((STAGE_NUM + 1)) echo "" echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${RESET}" echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD} Stage ${STAGE_NUM}: $1${RESET}" echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${RESET}" } ok() { echo -e " ${GREEN}✔ $*${RESET}"; do if [[ -f "$f" ]]; then SIZE=$(wc -c < "$f") ok "Found $f (${SIZE} bytes)" else fail "Required source file not found: $f" fi done # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Stage 2 — Compile # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ stage_header "Compile" mkdir -p /build OBJECT_FILES=() for src in "${REQUIRED_FILES[@]}"; do base=$(basename "$src" .c) obj="/build/${base}.o" info "Compiling $src → $obj" { echo "// compiled"; cat "$src"; } > "$obj" LINES=$(wc -l < "$obj") ok "Written $obj (${LINES} lines)" OBJECT_FILES+=("$obj") done # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Stage 3 — Link # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ stage_header "Link" PROGRAM="/build/program" info "Linking ${#OBJECT_FILES[@]} object file(s) → $PROGRAM" { echo "// linked" for obj in "${OBJECT_FILES[@]}"; do echo "" echo "// --- $obj ---" cat "$obj" done } > "$PROGRAM" TOTAL_LINES=$(wc -l < "$PROGRAM") ok "Linked binary written: $PROGRAM (${TOTAL_LINES} lines total)" for obj in "${OBJECT_FILES[@]}"; do ok " included: $obj" done # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Stage 4 — Package # ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ stage_header "Package" mkdir -p /dist ARCHIVE="/dist/release.tar.gz" info "Creating archive → $ARCHIVE" tar -czf "$ARCHIVE" -C / build/ || fail "tar exited with code $? — packaging failed" ARCHIVE_SIZE=$(wc -c < "$ARCHIVE") ok "Archive created: $ARCHIVE (${ARCHIVE_SIZE} bytes)" info "Archive contents:" tar -tzf "$ARCHIVE" | while read -r entry; utils!\\n\");\n}\n\n==== /build/program ====\n// linked\n\n// --- /build/main.o ---\n// compiled\n#include <stdio.h>\nint main() {\n printf(\"Hello from main!\\n\");\n return 0;\n}\n\n// --- /build/utils.o ---\n// compiled\n#include <stdio.h>\nvoid util_hello() {\n printf(\"Hello from utils!\\n\");\n}\n"}]}

The pipeline ran cleanly through all four stages. Here's a full breakdown of what happened:

---

## 🏗️ Build Pipeline — Results

### Stage 1 — Check Sources ✅
Both required source files were confirmed to exist before anything else ran:
| File | Size |
|---|---|
| `/src/main.c` | 82 bytes |
| `/src/utils.c` | 76 bytes |

If either file were missing, the `fail()` function would have immediately printed an error and exited with a non-zero code — stopping the pipeline dead.

---

### Stage 2 — Compile ✅
Each `.c` file was "compiled" by prepending `// compiled` and copying it to `/build/` with a `.o` extension:
- `/src/main.c` → `/build/main.o` (6 lines)
- `/src/utils.c` → `/build/utils.o` (5 lines)

---

### Stage 3 — Link ✅
Both `.o` object files were concatenated into `/build/program` with a `// linked` header at the top, followed by each object's content clearly delimited by `// --- /build/main.o ---` markers. The final linked binary is **16 lines** total.

---

### Stage 4 — Package ✅
The entire `/build/` directory was compressed into `/dist/release.tar.gz` (475 bytes) via `tar -czf`. The archive contains:
```
build/
build/main.o
build/utils.o
build/program
```

---

### Key Design Choices
- **`set -euo pipefail`** at the top ensures the script halts on *any* unexpected error, not just the ones we explicitly check.
- **`fail()`** is a centralized error handler — it prints the offending stage's message and calls `exit 1`, so no downstream stage can run after a failure.
- The pipeline is **data-driven**: adding a new `.c` file to `REQUIRED_FILES` automatically propagates it through compile and link with no other changes needed.

tools: cat /project/deps.txt, cat > /project/build.sh << 'EOF' #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail DEPS="/project/deps.txt" OUT="/project/out" mkdir -p "$OUT" declare -A DEPMAP declare -A SEEN ORDER=() # Parse deps.txt while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do # skip blank lines [[ -z "${line// /}" ]] && continue target="${line%%:*}" rest="${line#*:}" target="${target// /}" DEPMAP["$target"]="$rest" # ensure target exists in seen-tracking : "${SEEN[$target]:=0}" done < "$DEPS" # Topological sort via DFS visit() { local node="$1" case "${SEEN[$node]:-0}" in 1) return 0 ;; # done 2) echo "Cycle detected at $node" >&2; exit 1 ;; esac SEEN["$node"]=2 # in-progress local deps="${DEPMAP[$node]:-}" for d in $deps; do visit "$d" done SEEN["$node"]=1 # done ORDER+=("$node") } for t in "${!DEPMAP[@]}"; do visit "$t" done # Build in order for t in "${ORDER[@]}"; do echo "built: $t" > "$OUT/$t" done # Print build order echo "Build order: ${ORDER[*]}" EOF chmod +x /project/build.sh echo "created", /project/build.sh echo "--- out dir ---" for f in /project/out/*; do echo "$f -> $(cat "$f")"; done

metadata: bashkit={"dirs":["/dev","/dev/fd","/home","/home/user","/project","/project/out","/project/src","/tmp"],"last_exit_code":0,"tool_outputs":[{"commands":"cat /project/deps.txt","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":"app: lib utils\nlib: core\nutils: core\ncore:\n"},{"commands":"cat > /project/build.sh << 'EOF'\n#!/usr/bin/env bash\nset -euo pipefail\n\nDEPS=\"/project/deps.txt\"\nOUT=\"/project/out\"\nmkdir -p \"$OUT\"\n\ndeclare -A DEPMAP\ndeclare -A SEEN\nORDER=()\n\n# Parse deps.txt\nwhile IFS= read -r line || [[ -n \"$line\" ]]; do\n # skip blank lines\n [[ -z \"${line// /}\" ]] && continue\n target=\"${line%%:*}\"\n rest=\"${line#*:}\"\n target=\"${target// /}\"\n DEPMAP[\"$target\"]=\"$rest\"\n # ensure target exists in seen-tracking\n : \"${SEEN[$target]:=0}\"\ndone < \"$DEPS\"\n\n# Topological sort via DFS\nvisit() {\n local node=\"$1\"\n case \"${SEEN[$node]:-0}\" in\n 1) return 0 ;; # done\n 2) echo \"Cycle detected at $node\" >&2; exit 1 ;;\n esac\n SEEN[\"$node\"]=2 # in-progress\n local deps=\"${DEPMAP[$node]:-}\"\n for d in $deps; do\n visit \"$d\"\n done\n SEEN[\"$node\"]=1 # done\n ORDER+=(\"$node\")\n}\n\nfor t in \"${!DEPMAP[@]}\"; do\n visit \"$t\"\ndone\n\n# Build in order\nfor t in \"${ORDER[@]}\"; do\n echo \"built: $t\" > \"$OUT/$t\"\ndone\n\n# Print build order\necho \"Build order: ${ORDER[*]}\"\nEOF\nchmod +x /project/build.sh\necho \"created\"","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":"created\n"},{"commands":"/project/build.sh\necho \"--- out dir ---\"\nfor f in /project/out/*; do echo \"$f -> $(cat \"$f\")\"; done","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":"Build order: core lib utils app\n--- out dir ---\n/project/out/app -> built: app\n/project/out/core -> built: core\n/project/out/lib -> built: lib\n/project/out/utils -> built: utils\n"}]}

The build completed successfully.

## Summary

**Dependency structure** (`/project/deps.txt`):
- `app` depends on `lib` and `utils`
- `lib` depends on `core`
- `utils` depends on `core`
- `core` has no dependencies

**`/project/build.sh`** parses the deps file and performs a topological sort (DFS-based) so each dependency is built before any target that needs it. It also detects cycles. Each target is built by writing `built: <target>` to a file in `/project/out/`.

**Build order:**
```
core lib utils app
```

This ordering is correct: `core` (no deps) first, then `lib` and `utils` (which need `core`), and finally `app` (which needs `lib` and `utils`). Each target file was created in `/project/out/` with the expected content.

tools: cat /project/deps.txt, cat > /project/build.sh << 'EOF' #!/bin/bash # Parse deps.txt and perform topological sort declare -A graph declare -A in_degree declare -a all_targets # Read the dependency file while IFS=: read -r target deps; do target=$(echo "$target" | xargs) deps=$(echo "$deps" | xargs) all_targets+=("$target") if [[ -z "${in_degree[$target]}" ]]; then in_degree[$target]=0 fi graph[$target]="$deps" for dep in $deps; do if [[ -z "${in_degree[$dep]}" ]]; then in_degree[$dep]=0 fi all_targets+=("$dep") done done < /project/deps.txt # Remove duplicates from all_targets all_targets=($(printf '%s\n' "${all_targets[@]}" | sort -u)) # Calculate in-degrees (count how many targets depend on each target) declare -A dep_count for target in "${all_targets[@]}"; do dep_count[$target]=0 done for target in "${all_targets[@]}"; do deps="${graph[$target]}" for dep in $deps; do ((dep_count[$dep]++)) done done # Kahn's algorithm for topological sort declare -a queue declare -a build_order for target in "${all_targets[@]}"; do if [[ ${dep_count[$target]} -eq 0 ]]; then queue+=("$target") fi done while [[ ${#queue[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do current="${queue[0]}" queue=("${queue[@]:1}") build_order+=("$current") deps="${graph[$current]}" for dep in $deps; do ((dep_count[$dep]--)) if [[ ${dep_count[$dep]} -eq 0 ]]; then queue+=("$dep") fi done done # Create output directory mkdir -p /project/out # Build in correct order for target in "${build_order[@]}"; do echo "Building $target..." echo "built: $target" > "/project/out/$target" done # Print build order echo "" echo "Build order:" printf '%s\n' "${build_order[@]}" EOF chmod +x /project/build.sh , /project/build.sh, cat > /project/build.sh << 'EOF' #!/bin/bash mkdir -p /project/out # Parse dependencies and build a simple ordering # Using recursive approach with memoization declare -A visited declare -A building declare -a build_order build_target() { local target=$1 if [[ -n "${visited[$target]}" ]]; then return 0 fi if [[ -n "${building[$target]}" ]]; then echo "Error: Circular dependency detected at $target" >&2 return 1 fi building[$target]=1 # Find dependencies for this target local deps=$(grep "^${target}:" /project/deps.txt | cut -d: -f2) # Build dependencies first for dep in $deps; do build_target "$dep" || return 1 done unset building[$target] # Now build this target echo "Building $target..." echo "built: $target" > "/project/out/$target" visited[$target]=1 build_order+=("$target") } # Get all targets all_targets=$(cut -d: -f1 /project/deps.txt | sort -u) # Build all targets for target in $all_targets; do target=$(echo "$target" | xargs) build_target "$target" || exit 1 done # Print build order echo "" echo "Build order:" printf '%s\n' "${build_order[@]}" EOF chmod +x /project/build.sh , /project/build.sh, echo "=== Files created in /project/out/ ===" && ls -la /project/out/ && echo "" && echo "=== File contents ===" && for f in /project/out/*; do echo "$f:"; cat "$f"; done

metadata: bashkit={"dirs":["/dev","/dev/fd","/home","/home/user","/project","/project/out","/project/src","/tmp"],"last_exit_code":0,"tool_outputs":[{"commands":"cat /project/deps.txt","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":"app: lib utils\nlib: core\nutils: core\ncore:\n"},{"commands":"cat > /project/build.sh << 'EOF'\n#!/bin/bash\n\n# Parse deps.txt and perform topological sort\ndeclare -A graph\ndeclare -A in_degree\ndeclare -a all_targets\n\n# Read the dependency file\nwhile IFS=: read -r target deps; do\n target=$(echo \"$target\" | xargs)\n deps=$(echo \"$deps\" | xargs)\n \n all_targets+=(\"$target\")\n \n if [[ -z \"${in_degree[$target]}\" ]]; then\n in_degree[$target]=0\n fi\n \n graph[$target]=\"$deps\"\n \n for dep in $deps; do\n if [[ -z \"${in_degree[$dep]}\" ]]; then\n in_degree[$dep]=0\n fi\n all_targets+=(\"$dep\")\n done\ndone < /project/deps.txt\n\n# Remove duplicates from all_targets\nall_targets=($(printf '%s\\n' \"${all_targets[@]}\" | sort -u))\n\n# Calculate in-degrees (count how many targets depend on each target)\ndeclare -A dep_count\nfor target in \"${all_targets[@]}\"; do\n dep_count[$target]=0\ndone\n\nfor target in \"${all_targets[@]}\"; do\n deps=\"${graph[$target]}\"\n for dep in $deps; do\n ((dep_count[$dep]++))\n done\ndone\n\n# Kahn's algorithm for topological sort\ndeclare -a queue\ndeclare -a build_order\n\nfor target in \"${all_targets[@]}\"; do\n if [[ ${dep_count[$target]} -eq 0 ]]; then\n queue+=(\"$target\")\n fi\ndone\n\nwhile [[ ${#queue[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do\n current=\"${queue[0]}\"\n queue=(\"${queue[@]:1}\")\n build_order+=(\"$current\")\n \n deps=\"${graph[$current]}\"\n for dep in $deps; do\n ((dep_count[$dep]--))\n if [[ ${dep_count[$dep]} -eq 0 ]]; then\n queue+=(\"$dep\")\n fi\n done\ndone\n\n# Create output directory\nmkdir -p /project/out\n\n# Build in correct order\nfor target in \"${build_order[@]}\"; do\n echo \"Building $target...\"\n echo \"built: $target\" > \"/project/out/$target\"\ndone\n\n# Print build order\necho \"\"\necho \"Build order:\"\nprintf '%s\\n' \"${build_order[@]}\"\nEOF\nchmod +x /project/build.sh\n","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":""},{"commands":"/project/build.sh","exit_code":1,"stderr":"resource limit exceeded: maximum command count exceeded (10000)","stdout":""},{"commands":"cat > /project/build.sh << 'EOF'\n#!/bin/bash\n\nmkdir -p /project/out\n\n# Parse dependencies and build a simple ordering\n# Using recursive approach with memoization\n\ndeclare -A visited\ndeclare -A building\ndeclare -a build_order\n\nbuild_target() {\n local target=$1\n \n if [[ -n \"${visited[$target]}\" ]]; then\n return 0\n fi\n \n if [[ -n \"${building[$target]}\" ]]; then\n echo \"Error: Circular dependency detected at $target\" >&2\n return 1\n fi\n \n building[$target]=1\n \n # Find dependencies for this target\n local deps=$(grep \"^${target}:\" /project/deps.txt | cut -d: -f2)\n \n # Build dependencies first\n for dep in $deps; do\n build_target \"$dep\" || return 1\n done\n \n unset building[$target]\n \n # Now build this target\n echo \"Building $target...\"\n echo \"built: $target\" > \"/project/out/$target\"\n \n visited[$target]=1\n build_order+=(\"$target\")\n}\n\n# Get all targets\nall_targets=$(cut -d: -f1 /project/deps.txt | sort -u)\n\n# Build all targets\nfor target in $all_targets; do\n target=$(echo \"$target\" | xargs)\n build_target \"$target\" || exit 1\ndone\n\n# Print build order\necho \"\"\necho \"Build order:\"\nprintf '%s\\n' \"${build_order[@]}\"\nEOF\nchmod +x /project/build.sh\n","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":""},{"commands":"/project/build.sh","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":"Building core...\nBuilding lib...\nBuilding utils...\nBuilding app...\n\nBuild order:\ncore\nlib\nutils\napp\n"},{"commands":"echo \"=== Files created in /project/out/ ===\" && ls -la /project/out/ && echo \"\" && echo \"=== File contents ===\" && for f in /project/out/*; do echo \"$f:\"; cat \"$f\"; done","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":"=== Files created in /project/out/ ===\n-rw-r--r-- 11 2026-07-11 22:01 app\n-rw-r--r-- 12 2026-07-11 22:01 core\n-rw-r--r-- 11 2026-07-11 22:01 lib\n-rw-r--r-- 13 2026-07-11 22:01 utils\n\n=== File contents ===\n/project/out/app:\nbuilt: app\n/project/out/core:\nbuilt: core\n/project/out/lib:\nbuilt: lib\n/project/out/utils:\nbuilt: utils\n"}]}

## Summary

✅ **Successfully completed!**

### Build Order:
```
1. core        (no dependencies)
2. lib         (depends on core)
3. utils       (depends on core)
4. app         (depends on lib and utils)
```

### What was done:

1. **Read `/project/deps.txt`**: Parsed the dependency file which specified:
   - `app` depends on `lib` and `utils`
   - `lib` depends on `core`
   - `utils` depends on `core`
   - `core` has no dependencies

2. **Generated `/project/build.sh`**: Created a build script that:
   - Uses a recursive depth-first approach with memoization
   - Automatically resolves dependencies
   - Detects circular dependencies
   - Builds targets in correct dependency order

3. **Executed the build**: The script built all targets in the correct order, creating files in `/project/out/` with the format `built: <target>`

4. **Verified**: All 4 targets (core, lib, utils, app) were successfully built with correct dependency ordering ensured.
FAIL bashkit_bash/build_script_generator@anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-627616 tok · $0.0000 · 45908ms · 10 tool calls
  • ✗ bashkit_expectations — 2/5 checks passed (weighted rate 40%)

tools: cat /project/deps.txt, cat << 'EOF' > /project/build.sh #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail DEPS_FILE="/project/deps.txt" OUT_DIR="/project/out" mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR" # ── 1. Parse deps.txt into associative arrays ────────────────────────────── declare -A deps # deps[target]="dep1 dep2 ..." declare -A in_degree declare -a all_targets while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do [[ -z "$line" || "$line" =~ ^# ]] && continue target="${line%%:*}" target="${target// /}" rest="${line#*:}" deps["$target"]="${rest# }" # may be empty string all_targets+=("$target") done < "$DEPS_FILE" # Initialise in-degree counters for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do in_degree["$t"]=0 done # Count how many declared targets depend on each node for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do for dep in ${deps[$t]}; do (( in_degree["$t"]++ )) || true # actually count t's own deps : done done # Re-compute correctly: in_degree[t] = number of deps t still needs declare -A remaining for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do count=0 for dep in ${deps[$t]}; do (( count++ )) || true done remaining["$t"]=$count done # ── 2. Topological sort (Kahn's algorithm) ───────────────────────────────── declare -a order=() declare -a queue=() # Seed queue with nodes that have no dependencies for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do [[ "${remaining[$t]}" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=("$t") done while [[ ${#queue[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do # Pop front node="${queue[0]}" queue=("${queue[@]:1}") order+=("$node") # For every other target that lists `node` as a dep, decrement its counter for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do for dep in ${deps[$t]}; do if [[ "$dep" == "$node" ]]; then (( remaining["$t"]-- )) || true [[ "${remaining[$t]}" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=("$t") fi done done done # Cycle / missing-dep check if [[ ${#order[@]} -ne ${#all_targets[@]} ]]; then echo "ERROR: cycle or unresolved dependency detected – cannot determine build order." >&2 exit 1 fi # ── 3. Build each target in order ────────────────────────────────────────── echo "Build order: ${order[*]}" echo "──────────────────────────────" for target in "${order[@]}"; do echo " Building $target …" echo "built: $target" > "$OUT_DIR/$target" echo " ✔ $OUT_DIR/$target written" done echo "──────────────────────────────" echo "All targets built successfully." EOF chmod +x /project/build.sh, bash /project/build.sh echo "" echo "── Verify output files ───────────────────────" for f in /project/out/*; do echo "$f → $(cat "$f")" done, # Debug: check how the file was parsed while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do echo "LINE: [$line]" target="${line%%:*}" target="${target// /}" echo " target=[$target]" done < /project/deps.txt, # The issue: all_targets is populated inside the while loop's subshell (pipe). # Fix: use process substitution instead of a pipe so variables persist in the current shell. cat << 'EOF' > /project/build.sh #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail DEPS_FILE="/project/deps.txt" OUT_DIR="/project/out" mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR" # ── 1. Parse deps.txt ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── declare -A deps # deps[target]="dep1 dep2 ..." declare -a all_targets # Use process substitution (< <(...)) so the while loop runs in *this* shell, # keeping array assignments visible after the loop. while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do [[ -z "$line" || "$line" =~ ^# ]] && continue target="${line%%:*}" target="${target//[[:space:]]/}" rest="${line#*:}" rest="${rest# }" # strip leading space; may be empty deps["$target"]="$rest" all_targets+=("$target") done < <(grep -v '^[[:space:]]*$' "$DEPS_FILE") # ── 2. Topological sort – Kahn's algorithm ───────────────────────────────── declare -A remaining # remaining[t] = number of unbuilt deps for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do count=0 for dep in ${deps[$t]-}; do (( count++ )) || true done remaining["$t"]=$count done declare -a order=() declare -a queue=() for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do [[ "${remaining[$t]}" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=("$t") done while [[ ${#queue[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do node="${queue[0]}" queue=("${queue[@]:1}") order+=("$node") for t in "${all_targets[@]}"; do for dep in ${deps[$t]-}; do if [[ "$dep" == "$node" ]]; then (( remaining["$t"]-- )) || true [[ "${remaining[$t]}" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=("$t") fi done done done if [[ ${#order[@]} -ne ${#all_targets[@]} ]]; then echo "ERROR: cycle or unresolved dependency detected." >&2 exit 1 fi # ── 3. Build ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── echo "Build order: ${order[*]}" echo "──────────────────────────────" for target in "${order[@]}"; do echo " Building $target …" echo "built: $target" > "$OUT_DIR/$target" echo " ✔ $OUT_DIR/$target" done echo "──────────────────────────────" echo "All targets built successfully." EOF chmod +x /project/build.sh, rm -f /project/out/* bash /project/build.sh echo "" echo "── Verify output files ───────────────────────" for f in /project/out/*; do echo " $f → $(cat "$f")" done, # Deep debug: check associative array contents after the loop bash -c ' declare -A deps declare -a all_targets while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do [[ -z "$line" || "$line" =~ ^# ]] && continue target="${line%%:*}" target="${target//[[:space:]]/}" rest="${line#*:}" rest="${rest# }" deps["$target"]="$rest" all_targets+=("$target") done < <(grep -v "^[[:space:]]*$" /project/deps.txt) echo "all_targets count: ${#all_targets[@]}" echo "all_targets: ${all_targets[*]}" for k in "${!deps[@]}"; do echo " deps[$k]=[${deps[$k]}]"; done ', # Check bash version – older bash may lack certain features bash --version, # Test what actually works in this environment declare -A mymap mymap["foo"]="bar" echo "map: ${mymap[foo]}" declare -a myarr myarr+=("a") myarr+=("b") echo "arr: ${myarr[*]}" echo "len: ${#myarr[@]}", # Test reading file with process substitution declare -a lines while IFS= read -r line; do lines+=("$line") done < <(cat /project/deps.txt) echo "lines: ${#lines[@]}" for l in "${lines[@]}"; do echo " [$l]"; done

Parse deps.txt into associative arrays ──────────────────────────────\ndeclare -A deps # deps[target]=\"dep1 dep2 ...\"\ndeclare -A in_degree\ndeclare -a all_targets\n\nwhile IFS= read -r line || [[ -n \"$line\" ]]; do\n [[ -z \"$line\" || \"$line\" =~ ^# ]] && continue\n target=\"${line%%:*}\"\n target=\"${target// /}\"\n rest=\"${line#*:}\"\n deps[\"$target\"]=\"${rest# }\" # may be empty string\n all_targets+=(\"$target\")\ndone < \"$DEPS_FILE\"\n\n# Initialise in-degree counters\nfor t in \"${all_targets[@]}\"; do\n in_degree[\"$t\"]=0\ndone\n\n# Count how many declared targets depend on each node\nfor t in \"${all_targets[@]}\"; do\n for dep in ${deps[$t]}; do\n (( in_degree[\"$t\"]++ )) || true # actually count t's own deps\n :\n done\ndone\n\n# Re-compute correctly: in_degree[t] = number of deps t still needs\ndeclare -A remaining\nfor t in \"${all_targets[@]}\"; do\n count=0\n for dep in ${deps[$t]}; do\n (( count++ )) || true\n done\n remaining[\"$t\"]=$count\ndone\n\n# ── 2. Topological sort (Kahn's algorithm) ─────────────────────────────────\ndeclare -a order=()\ndeclare -a queue=()\n\n# Seed queue with nodes that have no dependencies\nfor t in \"${all_targets[@]}\"; do\n [[ \"${remaining[$t]}\" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=(\"$t\")\ndone\n\nwhile [[ ${#queue[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do\n # Pop front\n node=\"${queue[0]}\"\n queue=(\"${queue[@]:1}\")\n order+=(\"$node\")\n\n # For every other target that lists `node` as a dep, decrement its counter\n for t in \"${all_targets[@]}\"; do\n for dep in ${deps[$t]}; do\n if [[ \"$dep\" == \"$node\" ]]; then\n (( remaining[\"$t\"]-- )) || true\n [[ \"${remaining[$t]}\" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=(\"$t\")\n fi\n done\n done\ndone\n\n# Cycle / missing-dep check\nif [[ ${#order[@]} -ne ${#all_targets[@]} ]]; then\n echo \"ERROR: cycle or unresolved dependency detected – cannot determine build order.\" >&2\n exit 1\nfi\n\n# ── 3. Build each target in order ──────────────────────────────────────────\necho \"Build order: ${order[*]}\"\necho \"──────────────────────────────\"\nfor target in \"${order[@]}\"; do\n echo \" Building $target …\"\n echo \"built: $target\" > \"$OUT_DIR/$target\"\n echo \" ✔ $OUT_DIR/$target written\"\ndone\necho \"──────────────────────────────\"\necho \"All targets built successfully.\"\nEOF\nchmod +x /project/build.sh","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":""},{"commands":"bash /project/build.sh\n\necho \"\"\necho \"── Verify output files ───────────────────────\"\nfor f in /project/out/*; do\n echo \"$f → $(cat \"$f\")\"\ndone","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":"Build order: \n──────────────────────────────\n──────────────────────────────\nAll targets built successfully.\n\n── Verify output files ───────────────────────\n/project/out/* → \n"},{"commands":"# Debug: check how the file was parsed\nwhile IFS= read -r line || [[ -n \"$line\" ]]; do\n echo \"LINE: [$line]\"\n target=\"${line%%:*}\"\n target=\"${target// /}\"\n echo \" target=[$target]\"\ndone < /project/deps.txt","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":"LINE: [app: lib utils]\n target=[app]\nLINE: [lib: core]\n target=[lib]\nLINE: [utils: core]\n target=[utils]\nLINE: [core:]\n target=[core]\n"},{"commands":"# The issue: all_targets is populated inside the while loop's subshell (pipe).\n# Fix: use process substitution instead of a pipe so variables persist in the current shell.\ncat << 'EOF' > /project/build.sh\n#!/usr/bin/env bash\nset -euo pipefail\n\nDEPS_FILE=\"/project/deps.txt\"\nOUT_DIR=\"/project/out\"\nmkdir -p \"$OUT_DIR\"\n\n# ── 1. Parse deps.txt ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndeclare -A deps # deps[target]=\"dep1 dep2 ...\"\ndeclare -a all_targets\n\n# Use process substitution (< <(...)) so the while loop runs in *this* shell,\n# keeping array assignments visible after the loop.\nwhile IFS= read -r line || [[ -n \"$line\" ]]; do\n [[ -z \"$line\" || \"$line\" =~ ^# ]] && continue\n target=\"${line%%:*}\"\n target=\"${target//[[:space:]]/}\"\n rest=\"${line#*:}\"\n rest=\"${rest# }\" # strip leading space; may be empty\n deps[\"$target\"]=\"$rest\"\n all_targets+=(\"$target\")\ndone < <(grep -v '^[[:space:]]*$' \"$DEPS_FILE\")\n\n# ── 2. Topological sort – Kahn's algorithm ─────────────────────────────────\ndeclare -A remaining # remaining[t] = number of unbuilt deps\n\nfor t in \"${all_targets[@]}\"; do\n count=0\n for dep in ${deps[$t]-}; do\n (( count++ )) || true\n done\n remaining[\"$t\"]=$count\ndone\n\ndeclare -a order=()\ndeclare -a queue=()\n\nfor t in \"${all_targets[@]}\"; do\n [[ \"${remaining[$t]}\" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=(\"$t\")\ndone\n\nwhile [[ ${#queue[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do\n node=\"${queue[0]}\"\n queue=(\"${queue[@]:1}\")\n order+=(\"$node\")\n\n for t in \"${all_targets[@]}\"; do\n for dep in ${deps[$t]-}; do\n if [[ \"$dep\" == \"$node\" ]]; then\n (( remaining[\"$t\"]-- )) || true\n [[ \"${remaining[$t]}\" -eq 0 ]] && queue+=(\"$t\")\n fi\n done\n done\ndone\n\nif [[ ${#order[@]} -ne ${#all_targets[@]} ]]; then\n echo \"ERROR: cycle or unresolved dependency detected.\" >&2\n exit 1\nfi\n\n# ── 3. Build ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\necho \"Build order: ${order[*]}\"\necho \"──────────────────────────────\"\nfor target in \"${order[@]}\"; do\n echo \" Building $target …\"\n echo \"built: $target\" > \"$OUT_DIR/$target\"\n echo \" ✔ $OUT_DIR/$target\"\ndone\necho \"──────────────────────────────\"\necho \"All targets built successfully.\"\nEOF\nchmod +x /project/build.sh","exit_code":0,"stderr":"","stdout":""},{"commands":"rm -f /project/out/*\nbash /project/build.sh\n\necho \"\"\necho \"── Verify output files ───────────────────────\"\nfor f in /project/out/*;
